subject: Public Health
title: Cholera: Still a Threat
description: One-minute skill: Cholera: Still a Threat.

hook.text: Cholera can kill a healthy person within hours—through extreme dehydration from watery diarrhea.

buildup.text: Vibrio cholerae thrives in contaminated water—outbreaks still strike wherever sanitation collapses.

discovery.text: Oral rehydration therapy—a simple solution of salt, sugar, and water—has cut cholera mortality from 50% to under 1%.

twist.text: Cholera outbreaks surged in 2022-2023—spreading to 30 countries as climate change and conflict worsen WASH access.

climax.text: The ultimate cholera solution isn't vaccines—it's clean water and sanitation for all.

punchline.text: Cholera is a disease of poverty—it disappears where sanitation arrives.

quiz.question: What reduced cholera mortality from 50% to under 1%?
